lines.en_lit: the verse states: “Seven days shall he wear them, the one who serves in his stead from among his sons, who comes into the Tent of Meeting who is fit to enter the Tent of Meeting, And if it is so, he is fit
Rav Naḥman bar Yitzḥak said this is what is saying: anyone for whom the primary purpose of his anointment Tent of Meeting. excludes this for whom the primary purpose of his anointment is for war.
raises an objection the priest anointed for war serves neither in four like a common nor in eight like a High Priest.
Abaye said to him: you make him like a non-priest? Rather, like a High Priest due to animosity like a common priest, due to One elevates in sanctity and one does not downgrade.
Rav Adda bar Abba said to Rava: But this tanna who does not animosity does not serve
As it was taught The matters in which between a High Priest and a common The bull of the anointed priest, the bull brought for all the mitzvot, And the bull of Yom Kippur; And the tenth of an ephah

does not let grow wild, nor does he rend But he does rend from below, the common from above And he not become ritually impure his close relatives, And he is commanded to virgin, and he is prohibited from widow, And allows the return of the killer

And he sacrifices an acute mourner, but he may not eat and he may not take a portion And he takes portion first And first to sacrifice And he serves eight garments. And he is exempt from ritual impurity to the Temple and its sacred objects. And all Yom Kippur service are valid only by him.
And all these apply of many garments. except the bull brought for all the mitzvot,
And all these apply to a former High Priest except the bull on Yom Kippur and the tenth of an ephah

And none of them applies to the priest anointed for war, except for five items stated in the passage does not let grow wild nor does he rend And he not become ritually impure his close relatives. And he is commanded to a virgin, and he is prohibited from a widow. And allows the return of the killer in accordance with the statement of Rabbi Yehuda And the Rabbis say: does not allow the return
When has no animosity where is similar to him, inferior to him he does

Rabbi Abbahu was sitting and saying this halakha in the name of Rabbi Yoḥanan. Rabbi Ami and Rabbi Asi turned their faces Some say Rabbi Ḥiyya bar Abba said it, and Rabbi Ami and Rabbi Asi turned their faces.
Rav Pappa strongly objects to Granted, Rabbi Abbahu due to the honor of the house of caesar, But they should have said outright to Rabbi Ḥiyya bar Abba Rabbi Yoḥanan did not say this,
When Ravin came said: was stated being consulted That was also taught The garments in which the High Priest serves priest anointed for war is consulted
The Sages taught: How does one consult The one asking his face toward the one who is asked, And the one who is asked, his face toward the Divine Presence,
The one who asks says “Shall I pursue after this troop?” And the one who is asked says, Thus says God: Go up and succeed. Rabbi Yehuda says: He need not say Thus says God; rather, Go up and succeed,

One does not ask in a voice, as it is stated: “And who shall inquire for him And he should not think in his heart as it is stated: “And…who shall inquire for him before God” Rather, akin to the way that Hannah spoke in her prayer, as it is stated: “Now Hannah spoke in her heart;

One does not ask two matters simultaneously; And if he asks he is answered only one and he is answered only the first As it is stated “Will the men of Keilah deliver me into his hand? Will Saul come down?” “And God said: He will come down.” But didn’t you say answered only first David asked
